title: Prose Audit Finding (v1.1.0)
description: One finding emitted by a prose-audit module. A finding is a claim ABOUT the prose plus its adjudication against an authoritative source. It is never an edit, and it is never canon. Evidence state governs what language the finding may use (see PROSE_AUDIT_CAPABILITY_SPEC.md sections B and J). v1.1.0 adds the OPTIONAL evidence-first fields decision_state and claim_coverage (backward compatible: v1.0 findings without them still validate). Absence of contradictory evidence is NOT support: decision_state SUPPORTED requires affirmative authoritative evidence; 'not contradicted' maps to INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, never VERIFIED.

finding_id.description: Stable, unique, deterministic id. Convention: PA-<module>-<workkey>-<hash8> where hash8 is the first 8 hex of sha256 over (module + normalized_location + normalized_extracted_claim). Stable across reruns when the input is unchanged.

severity.description: Editorial impact if the concern is real. Independent of confidence.

confidence.description: Adjudicator confidence in [0,1] that the concern is real. For LLM adjudication this is the model-reported or calibrated confidence; never inflate.

evidence_state.description: Governs permitted claim language. See section B.

location.description: Where in the prose the extracted claim sits. Names only, never secrets.

excerpt.description: Short verbatim excerpt (bounded); the symptom in context. Never the whole chapter.

extracted_claim.description: The claim the prose makes, as extracted (by rule or by an LLM). This is evidence about what the text says, not a fact about the world.

authoritative_record.description: The canon/map/ledger record the claim was checked against. Null when the module is source-free (e.g. pure style-lint).

source_ref.description: Path or id of the authoritative record, e.g. canon/pages/geography/map_of_paratua.md or a map place_id.

record_value.description: The authoritative value that the claim was compared to.

concern.description: The contradiction or concern in one sentence: what the prose asserts vs what the authoritative record holds, or the textual symptom for a source-free module.

evidence_refs.description: Named, resolvable evidence for the finding: file paths, place ids, line refs, prior-audit ids. Never secrets, never absolute machine paths.

recommended_action.description: The smallest human editorial action recommended. Never an automatic edit. Human decides.

reason.description: Why this might be a false positive, e.g. figurative language, an alias, an intentional in-world exception, unknown map relationship.

llm_provenance.description: Present and non-null iff an LLM participated in extraction or adjudication. Absent for purely deterministic findings.

prompt_id.description: Stable id/version of the prompt template used.

structured_output_valid.description: Whether the model output validated against the expected output contract.

schema_version.description: Finding contract version, e.g. 1.1.0. Optional for backward compatibility with 1.0 findings.

decision_state.description: Evidence-first adjudication result (v1.1.0). Computed by the deterministic gate from structured evidence, NOT from a model's free-text verdict or confidence. Maps to evidence_state: CONTRADICTED->LIKELY (or VERIFIED with deterministic corroboration), SUPPORTED->VERIFIED, INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE->NEEDS_REVIEW/BLOCKED, AMBIGUOUS->NEEDS_REVIEW, NOT_APPLICABLE->FALSE_POSITIVE_RISK, ADJUDICATION_ERROR->BLOCKED. SUPPORTED requires affirmative authoritative support of every material component; absence of contradiction is not support.

claim_coverage.description: Per-component decomposition of a (possibly compound) claim (v1.1.0). Every material component is marked, so partial support cannot be reported as full support (coverage invariant).

evidence_ref.description: Resolvable authoritative reference (place_id/route_id) or null. supported/contradicted require a resolvable ref.

allOf.description: VERIFIED and FALSE_POSITIVE_RISK are deterministic-grade conclusions; if an LLM produced them alone they must be downgraded to LIKELY/NEEDS_REVIEW unless a deterministic check corroborates. Enforced in code, documented here.
